title: Validation Behavior
description: Understand how Bean Validation works with Spring.
question: A TaskRequest has @NotBlank on title and @FutureOrPresent on dueDate. If both are invalid, how many errors will MethodArgumentNotValidException contain?
options: 2 - all fields are validated and all errors collected
0 - validation is optional unless @Validated is used
Depends on the order of field declarations
explanation: Bean Validation validates all constrained fields and collects all violations before throwing the exception. It does not stop at the first failure. When @Valid is used on a @RequestBody parameter, Spring automatically triggers validation and wraps all errors in a MethodArgumentNotValidException, which in this case will contain 2 field errors.
